title: remote podman build host path propagation with out of context build files (nTar)
body:
### Issue Description

A remote podman build tars the context dir and any out-of-context inputs and ships it to the server. These out-of-context inputs are tarred while retaining their absolute paths, leading to host path (_only the path_) leaks in the build context dir extracted on the server end. These paths can further end up in the image in presence of a `COPY . /` in the Containerfile.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
./bin/podman --root /tmp/proot --runroot /tmp/prun \
    system service --time=0 unix:///tmp/p.sock &
sleep 1

mkdir -p /tmp/ctx /tmp/outside
touch /tmp/ctx/somefile
printf 'FROM alpine\nCOPY . /testdir/\nRUN find /testdir/\n' > /tmp/outside/Containerfile

./bin/podman --url unix:///tmp/p.sock build -f /tmp/outside/Containerfile /tmp/ctx
```

### Describe the results you received

Server can "see" host paths that ideally shouldn't have propagated:

```
STEP 1/3: FROM alpine
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:55afa1ecc21d2bb5e5045f32dafee56272ffd89860bac26f6c32123439af26a4
Copying config sha256:d529dd0c6e5597ac7e4a3e2dea65c3fcc6173f4cae713c409265c1dd9914a11b
Writing manifest to image destination
STEP 2/3: COPY . /testdir/
--> 3227d6dfbed5
STEP 3/3: RUN find /testdir/
/testdir/
/testdir/tmp
/testdir/tmp/outside
/testdir/tmp/outside/Containerfile
/testdir/somefile
COMMIT
--> e5ec40a2aacb
e5ec40a2aacba2140dbd7d59507e6606117792fd4bc61d1a17d1bde94c1c207c
```

### Describe the results you expected

For starters, not the absolute host paths while building on the server, and worst case, the built image.

For a fix, I tried playing around with prepending the basename with a constant path prefix and the input index (to avoid conflicts). For instance, in the above example, instead of tarring up the Containerfile as `/tmp/outside/Containerfile`, the client passes on `.podman-build-external/{index}/Containerfile`

Subsequently the server extracts these path prefixed to a new /tmp directory in order to avoid `COPY . /` polluting the image with out-of-context inputs, given that they weren't ever part of the context dir to begin with.

I'd like to discuss this issue, approach and other approaches I might've missed before raising a PR.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

openSUSE Tumbleweed-Slowroll (6.18.9)

### Additional information

This was identified for `--ignorefile` by @Honny1 as part of https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25161
